summary: Do not ship yet: the e2e isolation/warm-up changes still do not guarantee the intended behavior, and the new skeleton can visibly break narrow-route rendering during loading.

findings.title: `reuseExistingServer` bypasses the new `.next-e2e` isolation guarantee
findings.recommendation: Set `reuseExistingServer: false` for this sandboxed suite, or add a separate readiness/provenance check that refuses to reuse any server not started with `NEXT_DIST_DIR=.next-e2e` and the sandbox env vars. As written, an already-running server on port 3401 will be reused with whatever dist dir and home paths it was launched with, so the suite can still hit a process using `.next` and defeat the stated anti-poisoning protection.

findings.title: Global setup silently accepts a failed warm-up, so the suite can still proceed cold and flaky
findings.recommendation: Fail `globalSetup` when the warm-up never succeeds instead of only logging a warning. The current code continues after timeout/connection failures, which means the intended post-reseed `/api/compositions` warm-up is not actually enforced and the first spec can still race the expensive cold reconcile path.

findings.title: `PageSkeleton` uses fixed 340px/460px lines that can overflow mobile-width shell routes during loading
findings.recommendation: Make skeleton widths responsive with percentages/max-widths instead of fixed pixel widths. On narrow viewports, the 460px line exceeds the content width and can introduce horizontal overflow while the route is loading, which conflicts with the acceptance bar that shell routes render cleanly.
